title: Thoughts on the Asuka mindrape scene
display_title: Thoughts on the Asuka mindrape scene
wikitext:
[]: Thoughts on the Asuka mindrape scene...             Quote Post by Cross of Iron » Sun May 29, 2011 11:27 pm When she goes into the rant against Shinji at the end of the mindrape sequence (You won't help me, you won't even hold me, etc.) and we see screenshots of her alone and dejected, it reminded me of the "Wall of Jericho" scene. What if Shinji DID go through the door when Asuka warned him not to? Would she have welcomed him, or would she just clobber him? Given her demeanor, I assumed she would have hit him, but recall the shot of her in her room during the mindrape scene alone by herself. Thoughts?

[]: Re: Thoughts on the Asuka mindrape scene...             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Mon May 30, 2011 12:07 am Cross of Iron wrote: When she goes into the rant against Shinji at the end of the mindrape sequence (You won't help me, you won't even hold me, etc.) and we see screenshots of her alone and dejected, it reminded me of the "Wall of Jericho" scene. What if Shinji DID go through the door when Asuka warned him not to? Would she have welcomed him, or would she just clobber him? Given her demeanor, I assumed she would have hit him, but recall the shot of her in her room during the mindrape scene alone by herself. Thoughts?

[]: Re: Thoughts on the Asuka mindrape scene...             Quote Post by Someone » Mon May 30, 2011 12:25 am Tarnsman wrote: Best case scenario? Weeks, upon weeks of sex. Worst case scenario? Asuka hits Shinji, calls him a pervert, throws a tantrum, gets angry that he doesn't see that she wants him, and goes back to sulking, while Shinji wonders what the he did wrong.

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Mon May 30, 2011 12:37 am Honestly, sex wouldn't happen. Both of them are far too reserved (even Asuka, no matter how much of a tease she might be) for that. Best case scenario would happen if he moved into that room with the smooth-talk (which he doesn't actually have in him) and then stand up to her when she rages a bit. Then she might, well, not have sex but maybe start seeing him in better light. EDIT: oh yeah, they are also both in their early teens.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon May 30, 2011 12:40 am Asuka was in Misato's room. But had "Third" checked on "Soryu", maybe he'd (still oblivously) comment on needing to sleep in the same room for the unision training. If Asuka'd be pissed or happy (eg: Flirt that they could spoon in Misato's room or something) seriously depends on her mood... But she seemed pretty needy then so she could have talked Shinji into things and B.S.ed it's for the training/synch tests. Maybe the kiss with a hug? (and more?) several episodes early?

[]:             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Mon May 30, 2011 12:43 am BiQ wrote: Honestly, sex wouldn't happen. Both of them are far too reserved (even Asuka, no matter how much of a tease she might be) for that. Best case scenario would happen if he moved into that room with the smooth-talk (which he doesn't actually have in him) and then stand up to her when she rages a bit. Then she might, well, not have sex but maybe start seeing him in better light. EDIT: oh yeah, they are also both in their early teens.

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Mon May 30, 2011 12:45 am I'm still not convinced that wasn't really just talk from her side as she could trust him to not actually take her up on that offer... Either way, Shinji wouldn't do it at that point, hot redhead or not.

[]:             Quote Post by illustriusCheese » Mon May 30, 2011 12:47 am I think they would have had a talk about why they pilot Eva instead of anything sexual happening.

[]:             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Mon May 30, 2011 12:56 am BiQ wrote: I'm still not convinced that wasn't really just talk from her side as she could trust him to not actually take her up on that offer... Either way, Shinji wouldn't do it at that point, hot redhead or not.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon May 30, 2011 2:18 am He'd have gotten an earful and perhaps a bruise, and she'd have gone to sleep a little happier, secure in the knowledge that she's isn't a complete failure at flirting.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Mon May 30, 2011 3:11 am Tarnsman wrote: Asuka pretty much propositioned Kaji for sex at the beginning of 22'. Emotionally vulnerable teenagers don't always understand the difference between sex and love, and that they're not the same thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon May 30, 2011 7:01 am Legendary wrote: This immaturity is even reflected in that date she has where she ditches the guy. She probably ditched him because he wanted things she's not actually confident enough to give out.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon May 30, 2011 8:18 am Tarnsman wrote: Does he get possessed by Molag Bal and become the King of Rape?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon May 30, 2011 9:51 am She would have shouted at him, called him an idiot and/or a pervert and, if Shinji was particulary unlucky that Day, she might've kicked him as well. I don't think cupid's arrow had already struck Asuka at that point, she was simply feeling lonely, because duh, she's stuck in foreighn country without anyone or anything she knows... That's Asuka's tragedy - she wants ppl to leave her alone, but at the same time, she doesn't want to be lonely. That doesn't make it less unfair of her to expect poor little Shinji to read her thoughts if she rejects him all the time... Still, that particular montage is some nice example for the most evil ways to use irony, we suddenly get to see these scenes (and later ones) from her PoV, the bits we didn't see, those that lay open that she did return Shinji's feelings...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon May 30, 2011 9:53 am Azathoth wrote: It's Kaji she wants to be with. At this point she hasn't even admitted to herself that she sympathizes with Shinji. She ditched Kodama's loser friend because he was a loser, as far as we can tell (there is no reason to think a high school student who begs his friend's little sister for a date with the middle-school exchange student isn't a loser...even in Japan). She tries to get it on with Shinji because she's lonely and bored (which she admits) and because she sympathizes with Shinji's condition and wishes he was like her (which she doesn't admit). I don't think it's a specific wish to be with Shinji that drove her home - or at least that wish isn't necessary to explain her behavior.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon May 30, 2011 10:03 am NemZ wrote: . Shinji is right to be suspicious of her motives, as he's being treated as a relationship test dummy.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon May 30, 2011 10:06 am Other way around, actually... She's doing all the work and getting nothing in response, which is what starts to eat away at her next episode.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon May 30, 2011 10:10 am NemZ wrote: Other way around, actually... She's doing all the work and getting nothing in response, which is what starts to eat away at her next episode.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon May 30, 2011 10:33 am Tarnsman wrote: Simply entering her room in the middle of the night

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon May 30, 2011 10:36 am NemZ wrote: He'd have gotten an earful and perhaps a bruise, and she'd have gone to sleep a little happier, secure in the knowledge that she's isn't a complete failure at flirting.
